action.reasoning: Observation: Training crashed with RDKit RuntimeError 'RingInfo not initialized' in GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect (after loading data and starting FP computation on train set). No eval score after 6 iters; PyG segfaults previously, RDKit bond duplicate fixed last iter but now ring init issue. Val data loaded but unused, no metric computation. Analysis: try/except pass on SanitizeMol hides failures for some reconstructed mols from OGB graphs (missing ring info for Morgan FP); FP compute slow without full cache success. Adding UpdatePropertyCache(strict=False) + GetSymmSSSR(mol) initializes rings reliably. Adding val_dataset with cache, post-train AP computation (per-task with NaN mask via sklearn, mean AP), and logging to metrics.jsonl gives primary metric visibility. Predict.py needs matching FP fix. Decision: Update both files with robust FP func + imports, enhance train.py with val eval for avg_precision baseline, run 2 epochs (time-aware), submit_eval for first score and iter timing.
